channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Israeli strikes continue in Lebanon as oil prices rise amid stalled US-Iran talks
item.description: Despite a declared ceasefire, the Israeli military has expanded its airstrikes in eastern Lebanon, targeting areas associated with Hezbollah, signalling a continuation of hostilities in the region. Th...

item.title: CUET 2026 City Intimation Slip Release Likely Soon
item.description: The CUET (Common University Entrance Test) 2026 candidates are eagerly awaiting the release of the city intimation slip. This slip will inform students about the exam city where they need to appear fo...

item.title: Alexandre Boulerice Leaves NDP to Seek Québec solidaire Nomination in Gouin
item.description: Alexandre Boulerice, the sole Quebec Member of Parliament for the NDP, has announced he will seek the nomination for Québec solidaire in the Montreal riding of Gouin. Until the provincial election sch...

item.title: Three decades after democracy, South Africa’s promise of a better life remains unfulfilled
item.description: Thirty-two years after the end of apartheid, South Africa is still grappling with the gap between democratic promises and lived reality. The article reflects on how the early Reconstruction and Develo...

item.title: Central forces to be deployed in Bengal for 60 days post-election, assures Amit Shah
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah has announced that central forces will remain stationed in West Bengal for at least 60 days following the Assembly elections. This assurance came as he addressed a rally ...

item.title: DA says true freedom in South Africa depends on fixing crime and policing failures
item.description: In a Freedom Day message marking 32 years since South Africa’s first democratic elections, Democratic Alliance (DA) Federal Leader Geordin Hill-Lewis reflects on the gap between the promise of 1994 an...

item.title: PM Modi and Amit Shah make final campaign appeals ahead of West Bengal election phase two
item.description: As the West Bengal Assembly election campaign came to a close ahead of the second and final phase of polling, Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah made last-minute appeals to...

item.title: Light Rain Chances and Warming Trend Forecast for Southern California This Week
item.description: Southern California may experience scattered light rain this week, with the highest likelihood occurring Wednesday night, according to the National Weather Service. Meteorologist Ryan Kittell reported...

item.title: Xavi Hernández Says No Player Will Ever Match Lionel Messi’s Unique Talent
item.description: Xavi Hernández, former FC Barcelona manager, has expressed his doubts that football will ever see another player like Lionel Messi. Speaking on the YouTube channel of Brazilian football legend Romário...

item.title: Judy Nyawira Revamps Home After Seven Years, Shares Family Reaction
item.description: YouTuber Abel Mutua and his wife Judy Nyawira recently gave Kenyans a peek into their revamped mansion after nearly seven years. Judy shared that their brown recliner seats, which had been with them s...

item.title: Younger Australians Likely to Prosper Over Lifetimes Despite Early-Career Challenges
item.description: Research from the independent think tank e61 Institute indicates that while young Australians face financial pressures in their 30s, including extended education, HECS/HELP repayments, and superannuat...

item.title: UP Students Conducted Community Research in Toboso Without LGU Notification
item.description: Two students from the University of the Philippines, Alyssa Alano and Maureen Santuyo, were among 19 individuals killed during an alleged clash in Toboso, Negros Occidental on April 19. According to A...

item.title: Makini School: Campus Locations, Fees, and Contact Details for 2026
item.description: Makini School is a reputable private educational institution in Kenya with multiple campuses, mainly in Nairobi and Kisumu. Established in 1978 by Dr Pius Okelo and Dr Mary Okelo, it started as a smal...

item.title: Michael Jackson Biopic ‘Michael’ Achieves Record-Breaking Global Opening Weekend
item.description: The Michael Jackson biopic 'Michael' don scatter box office records worldwide, earning $217.4 million in the first weekend alone. For North America, the movie pull in $97 million, with around 6.3 mill...

item.title: German carmakers under pressure as China slowdown and local rivals reshape the market
item.description: Germany’s automotive sector is facing mounting pressure in China as slowing economic conditions and rising domestic competition begin to reshape one of its most important export markets. Speaking at t...

item.title: Boko Haram gives Nigerian government one-week ultimatum, threatens to marry off, kill abducted women and children in Kwara
item.description: Terrorist group Boko Haram has given the Nigerian government a one-week deadline to negotiate the release of 176 women and children abducted in Kwara State. The captives were taken from Woro and Kosos...

item.title: Rise of Early Head and Neck Cancer Cases in India: The Role of Lifestyle and Tobacco Use
item.description: Head and neck cancers are becoming increasingly common among Indians in their 40s, a concerning trend driven by tobacco use, alcohol consumption, areca nut habits, and changing lifestyles. Once primar...

item.title: Maharashtra Court Convicts BJP Minister Nitesh Rane in 2019 Mud-Pouring Incident Involving NHAI Engineer, Sentence Suspended
item.description: A court in Sindhudurg, Maharashtra has convicted BJP minister and Kankavli MLA Nitesh Rane in a case dating back to July 2019. The incident occurred when Rane, then with the Congress party, led a prot...

item.title: Albanese’s steady leadership masks lukewarm public support amid economic strain
item.description: Despite a turbulent political and global environment, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has managed to maintain a relatively stable position in Australian politics. Divisions within the conservative sid...

item.title: State Library of NSW marks 200 years with exhibition of 200 remarkable collection items
item.description: As the State Library of New South Wales celebrates its 200th anniversary in 2026, lead curator Elise Edmonds and her team have selected 200 intriguing objects from the library’s vast collection of ove...
